structuralBlockers: Requires willingness to relocate to Austin or Fremont; not addressable by self-study or remote work.
Demands production software engineering experience in real-time or low-latency systems; self-taught full-stack projects and AI tooling do not substitute at Neuralink.

alternativeTargetBranches.role: Software Engineer at smaller neurotech startup
alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Fits self-taught profile with strong Python/TS skills and agentic AI experience; many early-stage BCI or neurotech teams value rapid prototyping over production pedigree.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Lower compensation, less structured mentorship, higher risk of company failure, but faster path to relevant BCI experience without relocation.

alternativeTargetBranches.role: ML Engineer in healthtech or wearable devices
alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Leverages existing Python/ML prototyping and LLM orchestration strengths for signal classification and user modeling tasks.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Less direct neural interface work, lower domain prestige than Neuralink, but avoids relocation and real-time production requirements.

clusters.name: BCI and Neuroscience Fundamentals
clusters.description: Core domain knowledge to interpret neural signals and read primary literature without hand-holding from neuroscientists.

subSkills.name: Neural Signal Basics
subSkills.description: Understanding spike sorting, local field potentials, and electrode interfaces.

concepts.name: Action potentials and spike waveforms
concepts.description: Explain how neurons generate detectable electrical spikes and what a typical waveform looks like on an electrode array.

suggestedResources.title: Principles of Neural Science, 6th ed., chapters 5-7
Spike Sorting Fundamentals - Neural Signal Processing Course (MIT OpenCourseWare)

concepts.name: Local field potentials vs single-unit activity
concepts.description: Distinguish LFP frequency bands from spiking activity and describe when each is used in BCI decoding.

suggestedResources.title: Principles of Neural Science, 6th ed., chapters 5-7
Local Field Potentials in BCI - review by Andersen et al.

concepts.name: Utah array and Neuropixels basics
concepts.description: Describe electrode geometry, channel count, and typical recording characteristics of common BCI implants.

suggestedResources.title: Utah Array and High-Density Probes - Neuralink technical blog posts
Neuropixels Technology Overview - Janelia Research Campus talk

concepts.name: Impedance and signal quality
concepts.description: Explain how electrode impedance affects recorded signal amplitude and noise.

suggestedResources.title: Electrode-Tissue Interface Review - Cogan 2008
Neural Recording Signal Quality Metrics - Open Ephys docs

subSkills.name: BCI Paradigms and Literature
subSkills.description: Key experiments and decoding approaches that became production features.

concepts.name: Motor BCI cursor control
concepts.description: Summarize the 2D/3D cursor control results from Hochberg, Shenoy, and Nuyujukian labs.

suggestedResources.title: Reach and Grasp by People with Tetraplegia using Neural Interface System - Hochberg 2012
High-performance neural prosthetics - Shenoy lab reviews

concepts.name: Speech and language decoding
concepts.description: Outline recent intracortical speech BCI results and the feature representations used.

suggestedResources.title: High-performance brain-to-text communication - Willett 2023
Speech decoding from ECoG - Anumanchipalli 2019

concepts.name: Closed-loop calibration protocols
concepts.description: Describe how initial decoder calibration sessions are structured and why recalibration is needed.

suggestedResources.title: Closed-loop decoder calibration - Orsborn et al.
Neuralink Participant Session Workflow - public demos

concepts.name: Regulatory and safety context
concepts.description: State the difference between IDE, PMA, and the role of IRB in human BCI trials.

Medical Device Regulation Overview - FDA resources

suggestedArtefact.title: Literature synthesis: 5 key BCI papers turned into pseudocode decoding pipelines
suggestedArtefact.description: Write a 6-8 page document mapping published methods to implementable real-time steps.

clusters.name: Real-time Low-Latency Systems
clusters.description: Production-grade real-time decoding and control loops required for participant sessions.

subSkills.name: Real-time Architecture Patterns
subSkills.description: Designing pipelines that meet sub-50ms end-to-end latency.

concepts.name: Ring buffers and lock-free queues
concepts.description: Implement single-producer single-consumer queues for continuous neural data without blocking.

suggestedResources.title: Real-Time Systems: Design Principles for Distributed Embedded Applications by Hermann Kopetz
Lock-free Programming in C++ - Herb Sutter talk

concepts.name: Threading and scheduling for determinism
concepts.description: Use real-time priorities, CPU affinity, and avoid GC pauses in decoding threads.

suggestedResources.title: Real-Time Systems: Design Principles for Distributed Embedded Applications by Hermann Kopetz
Real-time Linux for Embedded - Embedded Linux Conference talk

concepts.name: Zero-copy data paths
concepts.description: Move data between acquisition, DSP, decoder, and UI without unnecessary copies.

concepts.name: Profiling and latency measurement
concepts.description: Instrument pipelines to measure p99 latency from spike timestamp to actuator command.
